intake.claim_to_verify: cli/run.hexa:583,604 — exec('date +%N 2>/dev/null | head -c 9') returns literal 'N' on macOS, collapsing entropy and defeating Day-1 counter-replay fix
intake.scope: investigate actual macOS behavior + estimate impact + design cascade fix; impl is SEPARATE cycle

F1_source_extract.line_583_purpose: build per-round salt string concatenating: urand_i64 #1 + 'x' + urand_i64 #2 + 'n' + 9-char ns + 'i' + round_idx. Salt becomes prefix of round_seed via _round_seed_rich() to scatter engine seed-hash buckets (fnv1a/djb2/sha64/entropy/length 8-slot bias_jitter feature space).
F1_source_extract.line_604_purpose: build sigma-resonance seed suffix: base + '#sr-sigma=<label>-r<salt>x<urand>n<ns>'. Suffix perturbs hash inputs for depth=1 resonance variants (sigma 0.01/0.05/0.10/0.20/0.40).
callers: cli/run.hexa:3443 spec_base_salt = _round_entropy_salt(round) — main drill speculation loop
cli/run.hexa:3457,3473 _round_seed_rich(nonce_base, round, rich_salt) — round-prefix salt application
cli/run.hexa:1624-1628, 3653-3674 _resonance_seed(round_seed, sigma_label, salt) — 5 sigma variants per drill round
F1_source_extract.what_is_protected: engine seed-hash bucket distribution across rounds AND sigma-resonance variants; goal is wider feature-space coverage so bias_jitter does not collapse to identical 8-slot vectors → identical engine outputs

actual_observed_on_this_macos.verdict: macOS 15.6.1 ships date(1) that DOES support %N (real nanoseconds)

F2_macos_actual_behavior.man_date_quote: '%N' for nanoseconds ... non-standard extension. It is compatible with GNU date's '%N'. The '%N' conversion specification was added in FreeBSD 14.1.
F2_macos_actual_behavior.honest_correction: On THIS macOS (15.6.1) the carry-forward claim is FALSE — entropy is NOT collapsed here. However, on pre-FreeBSD-14.1 BSD date implementations (older macOS, Big Sur and earlier likely; embedded BSDs; some minimal containers) the format spec is treated literally and emits 'N' (1 char). After head -c 9 + trim → 'N' (constant string), then ns_safe = 'N' (non-empty so the guard does not zero it). Salt component for that machine becomes constant per process invocation lineage — collapse condition realized only on those platforms.
F2_macos_actual_behavior.collapse_condition_precise: platform where date(1) does not understand %N → 'N' literal → ns_safe='N' constant per machine forever
F2_macos_actual_behavior.non_collapse_safety_net_present: ns.len()==0 guard sets ns_safe='0' but only triggers when stderr suppression yields empty string; literal 'N' has len==1 so guard misses it

F3_threat_model.day_1_counter_replay_bug_per_comment: cli/run.hexa:611-612 documents original bug: 'iter_26: round1 +617 → round2 +617 → round3 SIGKILL' replay — engine produced identical counter sequences per round because seed was identical, so the same crash was rediscovered on each round (no exploration, just replay).
F3_threat_model.salt_role: salt is NOT a cryptographic nonce in adversarial sense — it is engine-input perturbation to break determinism in bias_jitter's 8-slot feature hash so different rounds explore different parts of the seed-feature space.
F3_threat_model.actual_threat_class: exploration-coverage threat (engine determinism), NOT secrecy/MAC/auth threat. Adversary model is the engine itself producing identical features — not an external attacker forging seeds.
F3_threat_model.impact_if_ns_collapses: salt still has 2x urand_i64 (128 bits real entropy) + round index. ns_constant only loses ~30 bits of variability. r1+r2 alone almost certainly suffice for bucket scatter — but the original Day-3 fix (2026-04-21) explicitly added ns BECAUSE r1+r2 alone left observable bucket collisions ('round 2 == round 3 수치 완전 동일' per comment line 625).
F3_threat_model.honesty_caveat: this kick CANNOT verify whether ns contributes meaningfully beyond urand×2 without empirical bias_jitter measurement — the comment asserts it but does not cite numerical evidence. Treat as design intent, not proven necessity.
F3_threat_model.secrecy_dimension: NONE — these salts are not secrets, no MAC, no auth. Calling this a 'security regression' overstates: it is a coverage/exploration regression.

canonical_pattern_reference: tool/kick_bench.hexa:16-35 _now_unix_ms() — try BSD %s%3N → check N/parse-fail → python3 fallback → date +%s × 1000
hexa-lang/stdlib/portable_fs.hexa:41-50 pfs_now_ns() — try date +%s%N → check '!s.contains("N")' → fallback to date +%s × 10^9
F4_fix_design.proposed_helper_location: cli/run.hexa near line 570 (private helper) OR import pfs_now_ns() from portable_fs (already imported at line 3)
F4_fix_design.preferred_choice: REUSE pfs_now_ns() — already imported, already battle-tested for cross-platform, single source of truth for date %N N-guard
F4_fix_design.design_pseudocode: fn _ns_salt9() -> str { let raw = pfs_now_ns(); if raw.len() < 9 { return '0' } return raw.substr(raw.len()-9, 9) }  // last 9 chars = nanosecond fractional portion; on macOS-pre-14.1 fallback yields '<sec>000000000' → last 9 = '000000000' which is constant BUT the urand×2 already provides 128 bits — accept as graceful degradation; document explicitly
F4_fix_design.alternative_stronger: if 9-char ns degrading to '000000000' on pre-14.1 macOS is unacceptable, add python3 cascade: exec('python3 -c "import time;print(time.time_ns())"').trim() → take last 9 chars. python3 is already used by _now_unix_ms() so dependency is not new.
F4_fix_design.recommendation: Cascade: pfs_now_ns() → if last9 == '000000000' (BSD fallback signature) → python3 time_ns last9 → if that fails → constant '0' + urand×2 carries entropy. This degrades GRACEFULLY across 3 tiers without breaking Linux (tier 1 succeeds with real ns).
F4_fix_design.linux_safety_check: GNU date +%s%N returns e.g. '1777249290618555000' → last 9 = '618555000' (real ns) — IDENTICAL semantic to current behavior; no regression on Linux
F4_fix_design.macos_post_14_1_safety: BSD date now supports %s%N → cascade tier 1 succeeds; behavior MATCHES current good-path on 15.6.1
F4_fix_design.macos_pre_14_1_or_minimal_bsd: tier 1 emits literal 'N' → contains('N') true → tier 2 python3 → returns last 9 chars of time_ns → real ns; collapse FIXED
F4_fix_design.no_python3_no_recent_date: tier 3 returns '000000000' constant — but urand×2 (128 bits) still provides salt entropy; design explicitly accepts this as documented degradation, NOT silent failure
edit_sites: cli/run.hexa:583 inside _round_entropy_salt — replace exec('date +%N 2>/dev/null | head -c 9').trim() with _ns_salt9() helper
Add private fn _ns_salt9() -> str near line 570 implementing 3-tier cascade above
Update Day-3 comment (lines 574-580) to note macos-pre-14.1 graceful-degradation
Add @date-n-guard: cascade-validated comment marker for the lint sweep to recognize

F5_git_blame_regression_introduction.author: dancinlife
F5_git_blame_regression_introduction.context: Day-3 ROUND-SALT enrichment commit — INTRODUCED the date +%N shellout as part of broadening Day-1's single-i64 salt to multi-source. The commit added 'urand×2 + nanotime + round idx' to fix observed 'round 2 == round 3 완전 동일' collisions.
F5_git_blame_regression_introduction.regression_class: platform-portability oversight at design time, NOT later breakage. Author tested on Linux (where %N works) and assumed BSD parity. The %N support was only added to FreeBSD 14.1 (mid-2024); macOS port came later. Both lines 583 and 604 came from this single commit.
F5_git_blame_regression_introduction.lint_rule_landed: kick #12 (date_n_guard_lint) just-completed — rule that flagged this. Commit history: this is the FIRST sweep that would catch it.

F6_day1_re_verification_plan.scope: Day-1 counter-replay fix (2026-04-19 commits ec0ede1f1, 3440614c8) introduced _round_seed() with single-i64 salt. Day-3 (2026-04-21 ea78a25c7) ENHANCED it with multi-source. The Day-1 fix per se does NOT depend on date %N — it relied on a single salt: i64 from _urand_i64(). Day-3's enhancement is what the regression touches.
F6_day1_re_verification_plan.honest_reframe: Day-1 fix is NOT broken — _round_seed() at line 609 still works and does not call date. It is the Day-3 ENHANCEMENT (_round_entropy_salt + _round_seed_rich) that has the macos-pre-14.1 regression. The carry-forward incorrectly framed this as defeating the Day-1 fix; more precisely: Day-3's enrichment over Day-1 degrades to (Day-1 + 1 extra urand) on affected platforms — still BETTER than Day-1 alone.
re_verification_steps_post_fix: Step 1: confirm pfs_now_ns() cascade returns 9-char real ns on Linux (smoke: hexa run cli/run.hexa drill on ubu1/ubu2 → log _round_entropy_salt() outputs across 5 rounds → assert 5 distinct ns suffixes)
Step 2: confirm cascade on macOS 15.6.1 returns real ns (this machine — same smoke assertion)

Step 3: SIMULATE pre-14.1 BSD by stubbing date in PATH to one that emits 'N' for %N; run drill; assert _round_entropy_salt() still emits 5 DISTINCT salts across rounds (urand×2 entropy alone) AND assert ns component falls back via tier-2 python3 to real ns

Step 4: bias_jitter empirical — re-run the original Day-3 reproducer (the 'round 2 == round 3 완전 동일' case from comment 625) on a stubbed-pre-14.1 environment; verify post-fix that round-2 and round-3 numerical outputs DIVERGE
Step 5: regression smoke — run kick_bench across all backends 3x with the fix applied; verify dedup_ratio across rounds remains > prior baseline (i.e., enrichment effect preserved)
Step 6: tighten lint — extend date_n_guard_lint to assert ns-cascade pattern (presence of contains('N') N-guard OR pfs_now_ns() call) in any new date %N usage
F6_day1_re_verification_plan.re_verification_owner: next-cycle implementer; this design cycle does NOT execute — read-only per kick policy

diagnosis_honesty_gate.result: PASS — diagnosis grounded in direct verification (not assumption); honest correction made to carry-forward overstatement

design_history_reverification_gate.result: PASS — fix design uses existing canonical pattern (pfs_now_ns + cascade); regression introduction commit identified; re-verification plan covers pre-14.1 simulation

tier1_findings.summary: Carry-forward claim FALSE on this system; macOS 15.6.1 (FreeBSD 14.1+ port of date) returns real ns. Collapse only realized on pre-14.1 BSD / older macOS / minimal containers without GNU coreutils.

Salt is engine-input perturbation for bias_jitter feature-bucket scatter, NOT cryptographic nonce. 'Security regression' label overstates — actual class is exploration-coverage degradation. Honest framing matters for triage priority.
portable_fs.hexa already imported at cli/run.hexa:3 and exposes pfs_now_ns() with N-guard. Fix should REUSE not duplicate. Add 9-char extractor as private helper.

Day-1 _round_seed() (line 609) does NOT call date — it uses single i64 salt. Only Day-3 ENHANCEMENT degrades on affected platforms, and degradation floor is (Day-1 + 1 extra urand) — still ABOVE original Day-1 baseline. Day-1 re-verification need is LOW; Day-3 enhancement re-verification need is MEDIUM.
kick #12's date_n_guard_lint flagged the call but did not validate cascade presence. Extend rule to require contains('N') guard OR pfs_now_ns() reuse for any date %N usage.
head -c 9 on real ns yields exactly 9 chars (verified: wc -c == 9 — note: trailing newline not counted because head -c counts bytes from input not output buffer). No truncation bug.

carry_forward.directive: implement _ns_salt9() helper in cli/run.hexa using pfs_now_ns() + python3 fallback; replace exec at lines 583, 604; gated on author review of THIS design
kick #12's lint rule should recognize cascade pattern, not just flag raw %N usage
grep -rn 'date +%N\|date +%s%N' across nexus/ for any other unguarded sites missed by kick #12
the 'round 2 == round 3 완전 동일' Day-3 trigger should be archived as a permanent regression test fixture in state/test_fixtures/ — currently it lives only in comment line 625

honest_caveats: macOS version dependency: this finding is specific to 15.6.1; older macOS (Sonoma, Ventura, Monterey) behavior NOT verified — likely the regression IS realized there
python3 availability: the proposed cascade tier 2 assumes python3 in PATH; on minimal hexa-stage0 environments this may not hold — tier 3 (constant '000000000' + urand×2) is the safety floor
salt necessity for ns component is asserted by 2026-04-21 commit comment but not numerically demonstrated in this kick
no impl performed; fix design is READ-ONLY proposal awaiting author review (MT2 self-applied)
